prerequisites: A SignNow account with API access. Register a developer account at the SignNow developer portal and verify your email to reach the API dashboard.

An API application registered in the SignNow API dashboard. Each application provides a Client ID and Client Secret, from which a Basic Authorization Token is derived (base64 of client_id:client_secret).
A Basic Authorization Token to supply as 'api_key_id'. Find it in the API dashboard under the Keys section of your application, or build it by base64-encoding your Client ID and Client Secret.

A valid OAuth 2.0 access token (bearer token) to supply as 'auth_token'. Generate it against the POST /oauth2/token endpoint using the password grant (your account email and password) authorized with the Basic Authorization Token. The access token expires after 30 days and must be refreshed.
A 'start_date' value defining the earliest point from which the connector reads data.

authMethods.name: Basic Authorization Token (api_key_id)
authMethods.summary: SignNow derives a Basic Authorization Token by base64-encoding your application's Client ID and Client Secret. It is shown in the API dashboard Keys section. Provide it as the 'api_key_id' secret. It is used with the 'Basic' Authorization scheme to authorize generation of the OAuth access token.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 access token / bearer token (auth_token)
authMethods.summary: SignNow authorizes API requests with an OAuth 2.0 access token (bearer token) obtained from the POST /oauth2/token endpoint, typically via the password grant using your account email and password. Provide this token as the 'auth_token' secret. The token expires after 30 days and must be refreshed.

setupSteps.title: Create a SignNow account and open the API dashboard
setupSteps.body: Register a SignNow developer account and verify your email. After signing in, open the SignNow API dashboard (reachable via the API option from your SignNow home screen). The dashboard is where you manage applications and view your credentials.

setupSteps.title: Register an application and obtain the Basic Authorization Token
setupSteps.body: In the API dashboard, use the existing test app or add your own application. Open it to view the Client ID, Client Secret, and the automatically generated Basic Authorization Token. The Basic Authorization Token is the base64 encoding of 'client_id:client_secret'. Copy this Basic Authorization Token to use as 'api_key_id'.

setupSteps.title: Generate an OAuth 2.0 access token
setupSteps.body: Call the SignNow POST /oauth2/token endpoint to obtain an access token (bearer token). Set the Authorization header to the word 'Basic' followed by your Basic Authorization Token, and in the request body use grant_type=password with your SignNow account email and password. The returned access token is your 'auth_token'. Note it expires after 30 days and must be refreshed.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials and sync settings
setupSteps.body: Provide 'api_key_id' (the Basic Authorization Token) and 'auth_token' (the OAuth access token). Set 'start_date' to the earliest date from which to read documents. Optionally set 'name_filter_for_documents' to restrict the synced documents by name.

sources.title: How do I get my Access Auth Token in airSlate SignNow's API? | SignNow
How do I get credentials to try airSlate SignNow API? | SignNow
